criteria.label: Verify the two conditions (optimal substructure + overlap) before reaching for DP.
criteria.example: Fibonacci: f(n) is composed of f(n-1) + f(n-2) (substructure); both keep recurring (overlap) ⇒ DP.

criteria.label: Identify the minimal state and write the recurrence with base cases.
criteria.example: Coin change min coins: state = `target`, base `dp[0] = 0`, recurrence `dp[t] = 1 + min(dp[t-c])`.

criteria.label: Implement both top-down (lru_cache) and bottom-up (table) for the same recurrence.
criteria.example: Edit distance: top-down `@lru_cache` recursion and bottom-up `dp[i][j]` table yield identical results.

criteria.label: Pick the correct iteration order for bottom-up DP so every read is from a filled cell.
criteria.example: Knapsack: outer loop over items i = 1..n; inner loop over capacity c = 0..W.

criteria.label: Compress space when the recurrence depends only on a bounded window of previous rows.
criteria.example: Edit distance: keep `prev` and `curr` rows; drops O(m·n) memory to O(n).

criteria.label: Reconstruct the actual choice (path, item set, edit script) — not just the optimal value.
criteria.example: Knapsack: walk back through `dp` cells comparing `dp[i][c]` to `dp[i-1][c]` to recover which items were taken.
